originalTitle: Trump endorses New Jersey Republican not seen since March
summary: Tom Kean has been out with health issue since March. Democrats are sharpening their knives for general election
content: On the day Donald Trump endorsed him as a tireless advocate for New Jersey’s seventh district, the representative Tom Kean Jr was, as he has been since early March, nowhere to be found. Kean, a New Jersey Republican, was last seen when he cast a House floor vote on 5 March, and he is running unopposed in Tuesday’s Republican primary. The Democratic race in his district, meanwhile, had attracted multiple candidates and ample fundraising, with navy helicopter pilot Rebecca Bennett winning the primary on Tuesday. In late April, his office said he was dealing with a “personal medical issue” and would be back “very soon”. He told the New Jersey Globe last month he expected to return within “the next couple of weeks”. In the meantime, Kean’s social media accounts have continued posting regularly, with staff attending ribbon-cuttings and graduation ceremonies on his behalf. “Tom is working tirelessly to Keep our Border SECURE, Stop Migrant Crime, Grow our Economy, Cut Taxes and Regulations, Champion Small Business, Unleash American Energy DOMINANCE, Support our Brave Military and Veterans, and Protect our always under siege Second Amendment,” Trump wrote on Truth Social late on Monday night. The House speaker, Mike Johnson, who has reportedly spoken with Kean privately, has not been given a return date, three Republican leadership sources told CNN. Kean’s Republican colleagues, according to CNN, say they are worried about his health – and about holding a seat that four Democratic candidates, each having raised over $1m, had lined up to contest in November. Some have begun asking what New Jersey law would require if Kean were unable to continue after winning tonight’s uncontested primary. Multiple staffers in his office did not respond to a request for comment on whether Kean was aware that he had been endorsed by Trump, and if he is doing well.
chineseTitle: 特朗普支持自三月份以来未见的新泽西州共和党人
chineseSummary: 汤姆·基恩自三月份以来一直因健康问题缺阵。民主党为大选磨刀霍霍
chineseContent: 唐纳德·特朗普 (Donald Trump) 认可他为新泽西州第七选区的不懈倡导者，当天，众议员小汤姆·基恩 (Tom Kean Jr) 却不见踪影，自 3 月初以来一直如此。基恩是新泽西州共和党人，他最后一次露面是在 3 月 5 日在众议院进行投票时，他在周二的共和党初选中没有遭到反对。与此同时，他所在选区的民主党竞选吸引了多名候选人并筹集了大量资金，海军直升机飞行员丽贝卡·贝内特在周二赢得了初选。 4月底，他的办公室表示他正在处理“个人医疗问题”，并将“很快”回来。他上个月告诉《新泽西环球报》，他预计将在“未来几周内”返回。与此同时，基恩的社交媒体账户继续定期发帖，工作人员代表他参加剪彩和毕业典礼。特朗普周一深夜在《真相社会》上写道：“汤姆正在不懈努力，以确保我们的边境安全，制止移民犯罪，发展我们的经济，削减税收和监管，支持小企业，释放美国能源主导地位，支持我们勇敢的军队和退伍军人，并保护我们始终受到围困的第二修正案。”三名共和党领导层消息人士告诉美国有线电视新闻网，众议院议长迈克·约翰逊据报道曾与基恩私下交谈过，但尚未得到返回日期。据美国有线电视新闻网 (CNN) 报道，基恩的共和党同事表示，他们担心基恩的健康状况，也担心他能否保住四名民主党候选人在 11 月份排队竞选的席位，每名候选人都筹集了超过 100 万美元。一些人开始询问，如果基恩在赢得今晚无争议的初选后无法继续竞选，新泽西州的法律将有何要求。他办公室的多名工作人员没有回应关于基恩是否愿意发表评论的请求。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
